response.text:
# REFLECTION

The microgrid is in acute cascading failure with all peers islanded and critically depleted. r1c2 holds 37% SoC (1.48 kWh) against a 1.0 kW load, yielding ~1.5 hours of autonomy—marginally above survival threshold but insufficient to sustain meaningful outbound transfers. Both r1c3 and r0c2 are in critical distress (SoC ~33–35%), generating repeated urgent requests, yet every peer contacted (r1c1, r2c2) has rejected sharing due to their own survival crises. The network exhibits a classic tragedy-of-the-commons collapse: individual rational self-preservation (rejecting transfers) accelerates collective failure. Generation remains offline and grid reconnection status is unknown. The sole recent offer from r0c2 (0.0125 kWh) suggests minimal surplus capacity even among requesters. Continuing minimal transfers (0.25 kW/tick) to nearest peer r1c3 risks depleting r1c2 below safe margins; suspending all transfers preserves survival but abandons peers to certain depletion. The optimal strategy is to hold reserves, reject new requests, and escalate to DR aggregator for coordinated intervention—P2P sharing at this SoC level only accelerates synchronized collapse.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.40
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cascading microgrid failure; all peers critically low and rejecting transfers. r1c2 SoC=37% with ~90min runway insufficient for outbound sharing without risking own survival. Generation offline, grid status unknown. Escalate to dr_aggregator for coordinated intervention; P2P transfers will only accelerate synchronized collapse."
ttl_ticks: 6
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
